title: Dynatrace tokens should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

compliant.code:
# Retrieve Dynatrace token from environment
import os
dynatrace_token = os.getenv('DYNATRACE_API_TOKEN')
compliant.description: Using environment variable for Dynatrace API token

compliant.code:
# Load from secure configuration
dt_token = config.get('dynatrace_token')
compliant.description: Loading Dynatrace token from secure config

compliant.code:
# Using secrets manager
from aws_secretsmanager import get_secret
dt_api_token = get_secret('dynatrace/api_token')
compliant.description: Retrieving Dynatrace credentials from secrets manager

compliant.code:
# Placeholder documentation
# Set your DYNATRACE_API_TOKEN environment variable
# export DYNATRACE_API_TOKEN=dt0c01.XXXXXXXX.YYYYYYYY
